extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions. Reasoning: (1) sent becomes true ONLY via t2 (send_attempt with guard not opted_out); at the instant t2 fires opted_out is false, so the resulting state never has opted_out true while sent is set true. (2) opted_out becomes true ONLY via t1, which atomically sets sent:=false in the same update, so the transition into opted_out=true always yields sent=false. (3) opted_out is monotonic — no transition ever resets it to false. (4) Once opted_out=true, t2's guard (not opted_out) is permanently disabled, so sent can never be re-set to true; meanwhile t1, t3 (send_attempt while opted_out), and t4 (tick) all set sent:=false. Therefore in every reachable state with opted_out=true, sent is necessarily false. ticks only gates t4's increment and is irrelevant to the property. No counterexample exists at any execution length, including the 8-event bound.
